title: Chiller Fluid Velocity Limits
summary: Chiller selections often limited to maximum fluid velocities of 11 fps, and minimum velocities of 1.5 to 2 fps.
evidence_quote: Chiller selections often are limited to maxi-mum fluid velocities of 11 fps, and minimum velocities of 1.5 to2 fps.

title: Compound Pumping Advantages
summary: Compound circuits allow different water temperatures in system elements and hydraulically decouple circuits for simpler control and operation.
evidence_quote: They allow different water temp eratures and temperature ranges in different elements of th e system. Compound pumping can be used to vary coil capacity by controlling leaving coil air tempera-ture, which leads to better latent energy control.

title: Variable-Speed Pump Control Setpoint
summary: Pump controller setpoint is typically the pressure loss of sensed components at design flow.
evidence_quote: The controller is given a set point equal to  the pressure loss of the sensed components at design flow.

title: Variable-Speed Pump Power Reduction
summary: Motor power is reduced by the cube of the speed reduction, following affinity laws.
evidence_quote: Decreasing pump speed is analogous  to using a smaller impeller size in the volute of the pump, a nd the result is that motor power is reduced by the cube of the speed reduction, closel y following the affinity laws.

title: Minimum Pump Speed Limit
summary: Concerns exist about operating pumps below 30% of design speed for lubrication of seals and bearings.
evidence_quote: There are some con-cerns over operating pumps and thei r motor drives at speeds less than 30% of design, particularly about maintaining proper lubrica-tion of the pump mechanical seals and motor bearings.

title: Terminal Unit Classification
summary: Terminal units are classified as constant- or variable-volume and as pressure-dependent or pressure-independent.
evidence_quote: Terminal units are typi cally classified as c onstant- or variable-volume devices. They are further categorized as either pressure-dependent, where airflow through th e assembly varies in response to changes in system pressure, or pressure-independent (pressure-compensating), where airflow throug h the device does not vary in response to changes in system pressure.

title: Butterfly Valve Sizing for Modulation
summary: For modulating applications, size butterfly valve using Cv rating at 60° or 70° rotation.
evidence_quote: When sizing a butterfly valve for a modulating application, use the C v rating at 60° or 70° rotation; for two-position (on/off) ap plications, use the 90° Cv rating.

title: PICV Operating Pressure Range
summary: PICVs are rated for a differential pressure range (e.g., 5 to 50 psi) where they are pressure independent.
evidence_quote: PICVs are rated for a differential pr essure range (e.g., 5 to 50 psi) where the PICV is pressure indepe ndent.

title: Flow-Limiting Valve Operating Range
summary: FLVs limit flow within a predefined differential pressure operating range (e.g., 1 to 14 psi, 2 to 32 psi).
evidence_quote: The FLV will not allow the flow to exceed the flow limit when operating within the predefined differential pressure operat-ing range (e.g., 1 to 14 psi, 2 to 32 psi).

title: Equal Percentage Valve Application
summary: Equal percentage characteristic recommended for hot- and chilled-water coils to linearize output and stabilize control.
evidence_quote: This characteristic is reco mmended for control of hot- and chilled-water coils to linearize the output of the coil-valve com-bination, which makes control tuning easier and typically results in more stable systems.
